title: Review round 1: nie-07t E2E payment test
notes:
Confidence: 90%. Running out of real complaints: YES.

Scope: 1 assertion reformatted (client.rs), ~80 lines of receive-side test infrastructure added (mine-wait loop, bob scanner, spendable_notes assertions), doc cleanup in e2e_testnet.rs.

4 findings filed:
- nie-8lcm.1 (P1, task): Unnamed magic numbers 300s/120s/30s in mine-wait block. Real maintainability risk — the budget arithmetic is comment-only, not enforced. debug_assert proposal adds actual enforcement. Legitimate finding, though P1 is aggressive for test infrastructure.
- nie-8lcm.2 (P2, decision/GOOD): Positive observation on dual-subsystem oracle design.
- nie-8lcm.3 (P2, task): Reduce mine-wait poll interval 30s→10s. Thin but real engineering basis (saves ~20s tail latency on 300s budget). On the boundary of bikeshedding but not purely stylistic.
- nie-8lcm.4 (P2, decision/GOOD): Positive observation on exact-value note assertion.

Opinion labels: None applied. Both task findings have engineering basis that would survive a code review thread.

Missed bug risk: Low (~10%). The receive-side verification logic is straightforward sequential steps with no concurrency, no auth paths, no crypto. The primary risk would be a subtle timeout arithmetic error (mine-wait + scan > PAYMENT_TIMEOUT_SECS), which nie-8lcm.1 already captures. No security-relevant code changed. The assertion upgrade in client.rs is cosmetic only.
